title: Paginate Through All Results

problem.statement: A mock API paginates results: each page response looks like {"items": [...], "next_page": 2} or {"items": [...], "next_page": null} on the last page. Given fetch_page(page_number) which returns one page's dict, fill in the loop body of fetch_all_items so it follows next_page until it's null and returns the combined list of all items across pages.
problem.code_context:
def fetch_all_items(fetch_page) -> list:
    results = []
    page_number = 1
    while page_number is not None:
        ____
    return results

# 3 pages of 2 items each, next_page None on page 3 -> all 6 items returned in order
problem.use_case: This is the exact loop shape behind pagination for GitHub's API, most REST APIs with cursor/page-based results, and any inventory sync that has to page through hundreds of devices.

hints: Call fetch_page(page_number) to get the current page's dict.
Add that page's items to your running results list with .extend(), not .append() (items is already a list).

solution.explanation: Fetch the current page, extend results with its items, then advance using the page's own next_page value: page = fetch_page(page_number); results.extend(page['items']); page_number = page['next_page']. The loop ends naturally once next_page is None.
